standard_variable.semantic_intent: exact renal replacement therapy modality episode
standard_variable.semantic_grain: one row per retained labeled episode interval

approved_boundary_notice.reason: Amsterdam processitems exposes explicit start/stop intervals for itemid 12465 / CVVH and itemid 16363 / Hemodialyse. These can be emitted as exact modality episodes with standard labels CVVH and IHD, while access-line, peritoneal catheter, plasma exchange, orders, device parameters, and fluid-removal measurements remain outside the exact-modality episode output.
approved_boundary_notice.accepted_policy_scope: conservative exact modality positive episode boundary for CVVH and IHD

database_mapping.source_grain: one Amsterdam processitems exact modality interval row before exact-modality interval union
database_mapping.target_grain: one retained positive exact-modality episode row after merging overlapping approved intervals within stay and exact modality

identifier_normalization.subject_id_role: patientid joined from Amsterdam admissions_core by admissionid
identifier_normalization.hadm_id_role: not separately available in the current Amsterdam opening mapping
identifier_normalization.stay_id_role: raw admissionid is the Amsterdam ICU/MC local admission identifier and normalizes to stay_id under ICU/MC semantics

included_scope: processitems itemid 12465 CVVH mapped to std_rrt_exact_modality=CVVH
processitems itemid 16363 Hemodialyse mapped to std_rrt_exact_modality=IHD
excluded_statuses: 16352 Peritoneaal catheter peritoneal access catheter, not active dialysis interval
numericitems CVVH, Hemodialyse, and Peritoneaaldialyse fluid-removal or device measurements
adjacent_status_boundary.boundary_reason: This same-name variable represents exact RRT modality episode truth for CVVH and IHD under Amsterdam processitems interval evidence, not umbrella active-state truth, family active-state truth, fluid-removal measurement, order truth, device parameter truth, or renal phenotype truth.

episode_interval_build_translation.episode_label_translation: map Amsterdam processitems itemid 12465 CVVH to std_rrt_exact_modality=CVVH and itemid 16363 Hemodialyse to std_rrt_exact_modality=IHD
episode_interval_build_translation.episode_start_translation: use merged source start as Amsterdam relative support_starttime in milliseconds while also emitting relative_start_time_value in minutes
episode_interval_build_translation.episode_end_translation: use merged source stop as Amsterdam relative support_endtime in milliseconds while also emitting relative_end_time_value in minutes
episode_interval_build_translation.continuity_translation: merge overlapping or exactly contiguous approved intervals only within the same local admission and exact modality label
episode_interval_build_translation.parent_link_translation: link each exact modality episode to the largest positive-overlap std_rrt_active parent and to the corresponding CRRT-family or non-CRRT family parent
episode_interval_build_translation.no_row_translation: absence of a retained row means no positive exact-modality episode under this processitems source rule; it does not prove absence of umbrella RRT support

validation_contract.no_row_policy_check: absence of row is not universal proof of no RRT; only CVVH and Hemodialyse processitems intervals are mapped to exact modality episodes in the current Amsterdam opening mapping
validation_contract.source_code_lock_rule: all retained rows must originate from Amsterdam processitems itemid 12465 CVVH or itemid 16363 Hemodialyse
notes: This Amsterdam mapping is reviewed-approved for std_rrt_modality_episode.
The approved exact labels are CVVH and IHD.
Exact modality rows link back to the Amsterdam any-RRT parent and to CRRT/non-CRRT family parents.

execution_contract.formal_output_rule: Formal Amsterdam same-name output is approved only through the governed execution.py path reading this approved mapping spec and producing runtime plus rerun evidence.
execution_contract.non_bypassable_target_rule: Formal outputs should be generated only through the governed execution.py path reading an approved mapping spec and producing runtime plus rerun evidence.
